Gene Enrichment Analysis

Published

November 1, 2025

Gene Set Enrichment Analysis (GSEA)

Gene Set Enrichment Analysis (GSEA) was performed to identify biological pathways systematically altered by APOE2 overexpression in glial cells. This method evaluates whether genes within predefined gene sets are randomly distributed throughout a ranked gene list or concentrated at the extremes, indicating coordinated regulation of biological processes. All genes from the differential expression analysis were ranked by log2 fold change and tested against Gene Ontology Biological Process (GO:BP) gene sets from MSigDB for Mus musculus (minimum 30 genes, maximum 500 genes per pathway).

GSEA of brain tissue revealed 188 significantly enriched pathways (>30 genes /pathway, FDR < 0.01)

Reducing Redundant Pathways with collapsePathways

To reduce redundancy arising from overlapping gene membership between related pathways, the collapsePathways function from the fgsea package was applied, consolidating the results to the most significantly enriched pathway from each cluster.

Original significant pathways: 188 
After collapsing: 58

Results are reported as Normalized Enrichment Score (NES, indicating direction and magnitude of enrichment), adjusted p-value (padj, statistical significance after FDR correction), and size (number of genes in the pathway present in the dataset).

Key Enriched Pathways

The most prominently upregulated pathways were associated with protein synthesis and mitochondrial energy metabolism. Immune response pathways also showed significant upregulation. In contrast, pathways involved in neuronal function and synaptic regulation were predominantly downregulated.

All overexpressed genes in this pathway are ribosomal

Eef2, Rpl10, Rpl10a, Rpl12, Rpl13, Rpl13a, Rpl14, Rpl15, Rpl17, Rpl22, Rpl23, Rpl23a, Rpl24, Rpl26, Rpl27, Rpl27a, Rpl28, Rpl29, Rpl32, Rpl34, Rpl35, Rpl35a, Rpl36, Rpl36a, Rpl37, Rpl37a, Rpl38, Rpl4, Rpl5, Rpl6, Rpl7, Rpl7a, Rpl8, Rpl9, Rplp2, Rps10, Rps11, Rps12, Rps14, Rps15a, Rps16, Rps24, Rps26, Rps27, Rps27a, Rps28, Rps5, Uba52

All overexpressed genes in this pathway are mitochondrial

Antkmt, Atg5lrt, Atp5f1a, Atp5f1b, Atp5f1c, Atp5f1d, Atp5f1e, Atp5mc1, Atp5mc2, Atp5mc3, Atp5me, Atp5mf, Atp5mg, Atp5pb, Atp5pd, Atp5pf, Atp5po, Atp6-ps, Atpsckmt, Dnajc30, Ndufa1, Ndufa10, Ndufa11, Ndufa12, Ndufa13, Ndufa2, Ndufa3, Ndufa5, Ndufa6, Ndufa7, Ndufa8, Ndufa9, Ndufab1, Ndufb1, Ndufb10, Ndufb11, Ndufb2, Ndufb3, Ndufb4, Ndufb5, Ndufb6, Ndufb7, Ndufb8, Ndufb9, Ndufc1, Ndufc2, Ndufs1, Ndufs2, Ndufs3, Ndufs4, Ndufs5, Ndufs6, Ndufs7, Ndufs8, Ndufv1, Ndufv2, Ndufv3, Sdha, Sdhb, Sdhc, Sdhd, Stoml2, mt-Atp6, mt-Atp8, mt-Nd1, mt-Nd2, mt-Nd3, mt-Nd4, mt-Nd4l, mt-Nd5, mt-Nd6
